─[ Git Mv Gitignored Files ]─ ┐

name: git-mv-gitignored-files description: git mv fails on gitignored files — use cp + rm instead scope: internal user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Bash

Git Mv Gitignored Files

git mv only works on tracked files. Files inside gitignored directories are invisible to git, so git mv errors out even though the files exist on disk.

The Problem

A directory is listed in .gitignore (e.g., build/, node_modules/, .cache/). You try to rename or move files within it using git mv:

git mv .cache/old-name .cache/new-name
# fatal: not under version control, source=.cache/old-name

Git refuses because it has no record of the file. This also applies to individual gitignored files, not just directories.

The Fix

Use standard filesystem operations instead:

# Move/rename a single file
mv .cache/old-name .cache/new-name

# Copy a directory to a new location, then remove the original
cp -r ignored-dir/ new-location/
rm -rf ignored-dir/

Snags

  • git mv fails silently in scripts under set -e — the script just exits with no clear error message
  • Files can become gitignored after being tracked if .gitignore is updated — git mv still works on these until they are git rm --cached
  • Nested .gitignore files can cause surprises — a file may be ignored by a rule you don’t see in the root .gitignore
  • git ls-files can verify whether git tracks a file before attempting git mv
  • If you need the move recorded in git history, the file shouldn’t be gitignored in the first place — reconsider whether it belongs in .gitignore
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
